PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for and with Robert Fleming (Proposed Executor). Sharon Walker (Cousin of the Decedent) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it does not appear that the will was in possession of the Decedent and that it was entrusted to someone else. Mr. Carberry stated that he was not aware of any objections. Ms. Walker stated that she is not here to object to the will, that her cousin did not have the will, that it was entrusted to someone else and that they lost it. Mr. Carberry stated that he went through great length to find what happened to the will, that there was a civil elder abuse in which a lot of agencies were involved, that he thinks they gave the will to one of the agencies inadvertently and that the decedent did not have any papers in his room. The Court granted the petition for probate of lost will and for letters testamentary (subject to receipt and review of the order). Bond is set at $2,625,000.00.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
